meaning: n.<美> 庞然大物adj.<美> 巨大的
examples: Anything that merits the adjective "jumbo" is extravagantly large.
The game involves setting flight paths to safely direct jumbo jets to the large runway, smaller prop planes to the airstrip, and helicopters to the helipad.
It's all full of psychoanalytic mumbo-jumbo.
Instead, patrons can enjoy live music and New Orleans-style cuisine, from jumbo shrimp to jambalaya.
He said that stabilisers on all Il-86 jumbo jets in Russia would now be checked.
